page: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a bare-metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an Intel Xeon 1270 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs via hyperthreading, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 480 GB SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Performance benchmarks place this server primarily in the average tier for general compute tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield scores of 1728.79 and 8815.11 respectively, while the Passmark CPU score reaches 9737.8. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score ranks in the top tier at 3192.31, large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 31.11 GB/sec and 14.73 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is a strong point at 30.56 GB/sec. For database and web workloads, the server handles 8.08 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a static web serving throughput of 7.05 GB/sec.

Given the bare-metal architecture, this server offers dedicated resource allocation without hypervisor overhead, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared CPU performance. However, the weak large-block memory bandwidth and poor performance in medium-sized LLM inference, such as 16.42 tokens/sec for 7B model prompt processing, limit its suitability for modern AI workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, lightweight database operations, and legacy single-threaded compression tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a bare-metal server from the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by an Intel Xeon 1270 x86_64 processor running at 3.8 GHz. It features 4 physical cores, 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 480 GB local SSD. The server delivers balanced general-purpose performance, highlighted by a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 30.56 GB/sec and a top-tier Passmark memory score. However, uncached large-block memory bandwidth is weak, and LLM inference performance is poor on medium-sized models. With dedicated bare-metal resources and no hypervisor overhead, this configuration is highly suitable for static web serving, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose workloads requiring consistent CPU allocation.
og_description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a bare-metal server featuring an Intel Xeon 1270 CPU, 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 GB SSD, delivering balanced performance for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a bare-metal server with an Intel Xeon CPU, 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 GB SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare-metal server offering balanced compute, local SSD storage, and consistent performance for general-purpose and web serving workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 480 GB local SSD
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 30.56 GB/sec
Dedicated bare-metal resources with no virtualization overhead
Best suited for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications
